lines.english: The Beloved Lord God is his constant companion. ||1||
He dedicates his mind and body to the True Guru, and seeks His Sanctuary.
His greatest greatness is that the Naam, the Name of the Lord, is in his heart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋਗੁ ਹਰਖੁ ਦੁਇ ਸਮ ਕਰਿ ਜਾਣੈ ਗੁਰ ਪਰਸਾਦੀ ਸਬਦਿ ਉਧਰੈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: sog harakh due sam kar jaanai gur parasaadee sabad udharai |1| rahaau |
lines.english: He looks upon pleasure and pain alike; by Guru's Grace, he is saved through the Word of the Shabad. ||1||Pause||
He alone is the Lord's slave, who remains dead while yet alive.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਸਬਦੈ ਕੋ ਥਾਇ ਨ ਪਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: bin sabadai ko thaae na paaee |
lines.english: Without the Shabad, no one is approved.
He does his deeds according to the Lord's Primal Command.
Singing the Kirtan of the Lord's Praises, the Naam abides within the mind.
The self-willed manmukh wanders around the world in doubt.
Without any capital, he makes false transactions.
He Himself gives His gifts, without hesitation. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਣੁ ਰਾਸੀ ਵਖਰੁ ਪਲੈ ਨ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: vin raasee vakhar palai na paae |
lines.english: Without any capital, he does not obtain any merchandise.
One who serves the True Guru is the Lord's slave.
The mistaken manmukh wastes away his life. ||3||
His social status is exalted, and his reputation is exalted.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਪਉੜੀ ਸਭ ਦੂ ਊਚਾ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: gur paurree sabh doo aoochaa hoe |
lines.english: Climbing the Guru's Ladder, he becomes the most exalted of all.
Aasaa, Third Mehl:
O Nanak, through the Naam, the Name of the Lord, greatness is obtained. ||4||7||46||

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਸਮੈ ਕਾ ਮਹਲੁ ਕਦੇ ਨ ਪਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: khasamai kaa mehal kade na paavai |
lines.english: He never attains the Mansion of the Lord Presence.
Attached to duality, he wanders, deluded by doubt.
Entangled in worldly attachments, he comes and goes. ||1||
The self-willed manmukh practices falsehood, only falsehood.
Behold, the decorations of the discarded bride!

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੁਤ੍ਰ ਕਲਤਿ ਧਨਿ ਮਾਇਆ ਚਿਤੁ ਲਾਏ ਝੂਠੁ ਮੋਹੁ ਪਾਖੰਡ ਵਿਕਾਰੁ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: putr kalat dhan maaeaa chit laae jhootth mohu paakhandd vikaar |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Her consciousness is attached to children, spouse, wealth, and Maya, falsehood, emotional attachment, hypocrisy and corruption. ||1||Pause||
She makes the Word of the Guru's Shabad her decoration.
She who is pleasing to God is forever a happy soul-bride.
Her bed is so comfortable; she enjoys her Lord, night and day.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾ ਸੋਹਾਗਣਿ ਸਾਚੀ ਜਿਸੁ ਸਾਚਿ ਪਿਆਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: saa sohaagan saachee jis saach piaar |
lines.english: She is a true, virtuous soul-bride, who enshrines love for the True Lord.
Meeting her Beloved, the obtains eternal peace. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਪਣਾ ਪਿਰੁ ਰਾਖੈ ਸਦਾ ਉਰ ਧਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: apanaa pir raakhai sadaa ur dhaar |
lines.english: She keeps her Husband Lord always clasped to her heart.
She sees Him near at hand, ever-present.
My God is all-pervading everywhere. ||3||
Social status and beauty will not go with you hereafter.
O Nanak, he is absorbed in the True Lord. ||4||8||47||
As are the deeds done here, so does one become.
Through the Word of the Shabad, one becomes the highest of the high.
Aasaa, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਰ ਪੂਰੇ ਭਗਤਿ ਨ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: bin gur poore bhagat na hoe |
lines.english: Without the Perfect Guru, devotional love is not obtained.
The Lord's humble servant is imbued with devotional love, effortlessly and spontaneously.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕੈ ਭੈ ਸਾਚੈ ਸਾਚਿ ਸਮਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: gur kai bhai saachai saach samaae |
lines.english: Through awe and fear of the Guru, he is truly absorbed in the True One.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੇਰੇ ਮਨ ਹਰਿ ਜਪਿ ਸਦਾ ਧਿਆਇ ॥
lines.translit: mere man har jap sadaa dhiaae |
lines.english: O my mind, chant the Lord's Name, and meditate on Him forever.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਦਾ ਅਨੰਦੁ ਹੋਵੈ ਦਿਨੁ ਰਾਤੀ ਜੋ ਇਛੈ ਸੋਈ ਫਲੁ ਪਾਇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: sadaa anand hovai din raatee jo ichhai soee fal paae |1| rahaau |
lines.english: You shall always be in ecstasy, day and night, and you shall obtain the fruits of your desires. ||1||Pause||
The self-willed manmukhs lose their honor, and cry out in pain. ||1||
and the Shabad, the True Name, is enshrined in the mind.
Through the Perfect Guru, the Perfect Lord is obtained,
He becomes forever sanctified, and is absorbed in the True Lord. ||2||
One who bathes in the Pool of Ambrosial Nectar becomes immaculately pure within.
He sees the Lord God ever-present.
By Guru's Grace, he sees the Lord permeating and pervading everywhere.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਬਿਨੁ ਦਾਤਾ ਅਵਰੁ ਨ ਕੋਇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: gur bin daataa avar na koe |3|
lines.english: Without the Guru, there is no other Giver. ||3||
Wherever I go, there I see Him.
The Guru is the ocean, the perfect treasure,
O Nanak, the Forgiving Lord forgives us. ||4||9||48||
the most precious jewel and priceless ruby.
By Guru's Grace, the Great Giver blesses us;
Aasaa, Third Mehl:
Through perfect good destiny, one serves the Guru.
The Guru is the Ocean; the True Guru is the Embodiment of Truth.
